message:
fix: update provisioning tests for new notes field and renamed catalog
error variant

Adjust test fixtures in fcp-google-discovery provisioning to account
for upstream type changes:

- PolicyCatalogError::Deserialization renamed to
  PolicyCatalogError::Invalid — update error_catalog_variant test to
  use the new variant name and a more distinctive assertion string
- GoogleProvisioningScopeEscalation gained a required notes field —
  add notes: vec![] to all test fixtures constructing escalation paths
- GoogleProvisioningSurfacePolicy gained a required notes field —
  add notes: vec![] to the test fixture

Also sync .beads/issues.jsonl with latest task statuses.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45","title":"[GOOGLE] Discovery-Pinned Shared Runtime for Google Connectors","description":"Create a shared Google connector platform for FCP by extracting the reusable architecture from googleworkspace/cli and adapting it to FCP's deterministic manifest, capability, zone, and supply-chain model.

The outcome should be thin Google service connectors backed by common discovery, auth, HTTP execution, generation, testing, and provisioning infrastructure instead of one-off hand-authored per-service implementations.","design":"This epic is based on a deep reverse-engineering pass over googleworkspace/cli v0.7.0. The upstream design is valuable because it centralizes five concerns that we currently duplicate across Google connectors: discovery ingestion, auth materialization, generic request execution, helper overlays, and AI-facing schema/tool generation.

The crucial adaptation for FCP is that we must NOT copy upstream's runtime-mutating command surface literally. In a local CLI, it is acceptable to fetch Google's Discovery documents at runtime and let the available surface drift as Google changes its APIs. In FCP that would be the wrong tradeoff, because our manifests, interface hashes, approvals, capability envelopes, and operator expectations need stable, reviewable, reproducible behavior.

Therefore the FCP design target is: use Google Discovery documents as the source of truth, but pin them at build or release time; generate stable operation catalogs and manifest fragments from those pinned snapshots; allow handwritten overrides only where Google semantics do not map cleanly to FCP; keep one connector per service or service-family so least privilege and zone isolation remain intact.

This epic covers three workstreams: foundation crates/tooling, migration of existing Google connectors, and a first expansion wave for missing Google Workspace connectors.","acceptance_criteria":"- Foundation, migration, and expansion sub-epics exist and are dependency-linked.
- Every child issue is self-contained enough that an implementer does not need the original research memo.
- The graph makes the intended execution order explicit: architecture/foundation first, validation through migrations second, new connector expansion third.
- The backlog explicitly captures both what we want to reuse from googleworkspace/cli and what we intentionally reject for FCP.","notes":"Current repo context that motivates this work:
- The Gmail connector manually enumerates operations in connectors/gmail/manifest.toml and manually builds introspection in connectors/gmail/src/connector.rs.
- The Google Calendar connector has its own separate auth/config parsing and manual operation catalog.
- fcp-oauth already contains useful Google OAuth defaults (PKCE, offline access, prompt=consent), so we are extending an existing base rather than inventing auth from scratch.

Over-arching project goal served by this epic:
Reduce Google-connector duplication, make future Google connector work predictable and lower-debt, and ensure Google-family connectors fit the same FCP2 security/determinism philosophy as the rest of the platform.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:27:10.371735Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-07T06:07:33.444600Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","connectors","fcp2","google","workspace"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:27:10.371735Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":885,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Reverse-engineered upstream findings to preserve in the backlog itself:

1. googleworkspace/cli is fundamentally a Rust engine distributed through npm, not a Node implementation.
2. Its real power comes from a small alias registry plus runtime Discovery ingestion, not hand-coded per-method clients.
3. The executor is generic: schema-aware request validation, URL template rendering, uploads/downloads, pagination, and Google-style error normalization all live in one place.
4. Auth is a layered chain: raw token, credentials file, encrypted local credentials, default path, then ADC, with quota-project propagation.
5. Handwritten helpers are intentionally narrow and only exist where they abstract real complexity such as Gmail send/watch or Workspace Events subscription setup.
6. MCP tools and agent skills are derived from the same schema substrate rather than maintained separately.

Key FCP decision implied by those findings:
We should copy the shared substrate pattern, but not the runtime-mutating interface. Discovery should feed build/release-time generation for stable manifests and introspection, not live connector behavior drift.","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:29:26Z"},{"id":889,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Second-pass backlog preservation of source-level findings from the googleworkspace/cli reverse-engineering memo that were too compressed in the first pass:

1. Upstream packaging/runtime model: the project is a Rust CLI shipped through npm, not a Node implementation. We should preserve the compiled-native lesson and explicitly reject runtime dependency on npm/Node in FCP. Captured in flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.1.1.1.
2. Upstream control flow: identify service -> fetch/cache Discovery -> build dynamic surface -> reparse args -> auth -> execute. We should preserve the staged architecture but move surface generation earlier for deterministic manifests. Captured in flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.1.2.1 plus the existing discovery/generator beads.
3. Upstream service registry philosophy: keep aliases tiny and support explicit service:version instead of hardcoding entire APIs. Captured in flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.1.2.1.
4. Upstream auth breadth: raw token, explicit creds file, encrypted local creds, default creds, ADC, plus quota_project_id -> x-goog-user-project. Captured in flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.1.3.1.
5. Upstream generic executor specifics: param/body validation, URL template expansion, uploads, pagination, downloads, and Google-style error normalization. Already present in flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.1.4, with extra comment added below.
6. Upstream helper philosophy: helpers are thin overlays only for workflows that collapse real complexity. Captured in flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.1.5.2.
7. Upstream AI-facing generation: MCP tools and agent skills derive from the same schema substrate. Captured in flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.1.5.1.
8. Upstream setup UX: narrow default scopes and gcloud-assisted setup are architectural concerns, not polish. Captured in flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.1.8.1.
9. Local repo fit evidence: Gmail/Calendar currently duplicate exactly the categories of logic upstream centralized. Captured in flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.2.7.

This pass is intended to make the backlog itself sufficient as the durable research artifact, not just the high-level plan skeleton.","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:40:17Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.1.1","title":"[GOOGLE] ADR: use pinned Discovery snapshots for FCP, not live runtime surface mutation","description":"Write the architectural decision record that captures the central adaptation from googleworkspace/cli to FCP: Discovery is the source of truth, but FCP connectors consume pinned snapshots at build/release time rather than changing their operation surface at runtime.","design":"The ADR must explain the why, not just the what. It should explicitly cover interface-hash stability, manifest auditability, deterministic CI/release behavior, approval predictability, reproducible test fixtures, and the operational surprise cost of allowing live external docs to mutate shipped connector behavior.

It should also explain why this is not an anti-discovery stance. We absolutely want Discovery documents to drive the system; we just want them frozen at intentional update boundaries rather than fetched opportunistically inside connector runtime paths.","acceptance_criteria":"- ADR written and referenced from the foundation epic notes or project docs.
- ADR explicitly states the rejected alternative: a live dynamic all-Google connector.
- ADR explains how handwritten overrides fit on top of generated/pinned metadata.","notes":"Future-self reminder: this decision is one of the highest leverage parts of the whole plan. If we get it wrong, every downstream Google connector either becomes brittle and drift-prone or becomes over-manual and defeats the purpose of the shared platform.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MaroonMarsh","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:29:28.274996Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-06T15:07:27.899691Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T15:07:27.899667Z","close_reason":"Wrote ADR and referenced it from docs/STANDARD_Requirements_Index.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["adr","architecture","connectors","fcp2","google"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.1.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:29:28.274996Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.1.1.1","title":"[GOOGLE] ADR subtask: packaging and distribution stance relative to the npm-wrapper upstream","description":"Write the explicit ADR addendum for how FCP should learn from googleworkspace/cli's packaging model without copying the wrong parts.

Upstream is a Rust binary shipped through an npm wrapper. That fact matters because it proves the implementation is still native and compiled even when distribution uses JavaScript ecosystem tooling. FCP must preserve its own stricter rule: one Rust connector binary plus one manifest, with no interpreted runtime dependency leaking into execution.","design":"Capture three separate decisions so future work does not blur them together:
1. Implementation language/runtime: keep connectors as native Rust binaries.
2. Distribution ergonomics: decide whether any wrapper/installer conveniences are worth borrowing later.
3. Runtime behavior: explicitly reject any design that makes Node/npm part of connector execution or trust boundaries.

This subtask exists because the upstream repo name and npm presence can mislead future readers into thinking the approach is "Node-first" when the real architecture is Rust-first. We want that ambiguity removed from our plan space now.","acceptance_criteria":"- The ADR addendum explicitly records that upstream is Rust-under-npm, not a Node implementation.
- The addendum states what, if anything, we may borrow from the distribution story and what we must not borrow.
- Future Google connector work can cite this bead instead of re-litigating packaging/runtime confusion.","notes":"Preserve the precise upstream fact pattern from the memo: Cargo.toml declares the real binary and package.json is just the distribution shell. This matters because our local AGENTS.md forbids interpreted-runtime dependency as the primary connector model.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:38:05.228811Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-07T05:02:37.694839Z","closed_at":"2026-03-07T05:02:37.694823Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","fcp2","google","packaging"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.1.1.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.1.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:38:05.228811Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.1.2","title":"[GOOGLE] Shared crate: Discovery snapshot fetch, parse, normalize, and freeze","description":"Implement the shared Discovery layer that can fetch Google Discovery documents, parse them into stable internal types, normalize edge cases, and persist pinned snapshots suitable for generation and testing.","design":"The implementation should support both standard Discovery URLs and the alternate $discovery/rest pattern used by some Google APIs. It should preserve enough metadata to drive generation accurately: resources, methods, schemas, scopes, path templates, upload/download capabilities, and human-readable descriptions.

Normalization matters. We need a stable internal representation that removes nondeterminism, makes output ordering reproducible, and handles Google edge cases such as flatPath/path mismatches or inconsistent schema details. The goal is not just to deserialize JSON; the goal is to produce generator-ready semantic input.","acceptance_criteria":"- Shared types exist for methods, schemas, scopes, upload/download metadata, and resource hierarchy.
- Snapshot fetcher supports standard and alternate Discovery endpoints.
- Normalized snapshot output is deterministic and stable under repeated runs.
- Fixture tests cover representative Workspace and non-Workspace Google APIs.","notes":"Implementation bias: keep this crate pure and testable. It should not know FCP capability policy; it should only know Google Discovery semantics and snapshot normalization.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"PearlRidge","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:29:28.975326Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-06T15:54:26.555806Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T15:53:56.589209Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["codegen","connectors","discovery","fcp2","google"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.1.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:29:28.975326Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.1.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:31:19.516795Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":890,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.1.2","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Upstream detail to preserve here: the CLI does not hardcode methods. It resolves a small service alias table, supports explicit service:version, fetches/caches Discovery, and only then builds the command surface. Our adaptation keeps the resolution and normalization logic but shifts surface generation out of runtime and into pinned artifact production.","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:40:17Z"},{"id":906,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.1.2","author":"PearlRidge","text":"Implemented shared Google Discovery substrate as new crate crates/fcp-google-discovery and wired it into workspace members/default-members/deps. Delivered deterministic service resolution (alias plus explicit service:version), standard+alternate endpoint candidates, deterministic normalization pipeline (stable ordering across methods/resources/schemas/scopes), snapshot storage key helper, and fixture-backed tests for Workspace + non-Workspace APIs. Validation: rch exec cargo check/clippy/test for fcp-google-discovery passed; workspace rch exec cargo check and clippy -D warnings also passed in current tree (rch workers failed open to local). cargo fmt --check fails due widespread preexisting formatting drift outside this bead edit surface.","created_at":"2026-03-06T15:54:26Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.1.8","title":"[GOOGLE] Provisioning automation: OAuth setup, API enablement, scopes, and Pub/Sub lifecycle","description":"Define and implement the operator/host-side automation layer for Google connector provisioning so users do not have to manually stitch together OAuth clients, API enablement, scope selection, or Pub/Sub resources for event-driven flows.","design":"The upstream gws project is opinionated that tedious setup should be automated.  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.1.8.1","title":"[GOOGLE] Provisioning subtask: consent-restriction-aware scopes and gcloud-assisted setup UX","description":"Design the provisioning/setup UX so it respects Google's consent restrictions, keeps default scopes narrow, and automates API/project setup around gcloud where that genuinely reduces friction.","design":"Upstream auth_commands.rs keeps default scopes narrow and setup.rs automates project/API/scope setup around gcloud. Those are not incidental UX details; they are part of how the system stays usable without immediately requesting huge permissions or forcing users through brittle manual setup.

For FCP, this should become a provisioning rule set:
- narrow-by-default scopes
- explicit escalation paths when a connector/helper genuinely needs more
- automation for API enablement, OAuth configuration, and Pub/Sub prerequisites where applicable
- clear separation between provisioning-time credentials and runtime credentials

This is especially important for Gmail, Calendar, and Workspace Events, where a sloppy setup story will create user friction or over-broad auth requests.","acceptance_criteria":"- Scope-default and escalation policy is explicit.
- gcloud-assisted automation boundaries are documented, including fallback behavior.
- Gmail, Calendar, and Workspace Events requirements are called out directly.
- The bead explains why setup UX is part of the architecture, not a later polish task.","notes":"Preserve the upstream lesson here: Google's auth and consent surfaces are opinionated enough that provisioning behavior must be planned early, not discovered accidentally during connector implementation.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WildPond","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:40:14.426488Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-07T03:53:21.419119Z","closed_at":"2026-03-07T03:53:21.419098Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp2","gcloud","google","oauth","provisioning"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.1.8.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:40:15.853497Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.1.8.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.1.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:40:15.977456Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.1.8.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.1.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:40:14.426488Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

It should explain the motivation, architecture, update workflow for pinned snapshots, override philosophy, auth materialization boundaries, testing strategy, and service-selection rationale.","acceptance_criteria":"- A future implementer can add or migrate a Google connector without consulting the original planning conversation.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.2.7","title":"[GOOGLE] Baseline audit: Gmail/Calendar duplication, convergence map, and destination-format fit","description":"Freeze the current local baseline for Gmail and Google Calendar, record exactly where generic Google logic is duplicated today, and define the target split between shared substrate and handwritten service-specific overlays.","design":"This bead is the repo-specific grounding layer for the migration work. It should capture the exact local evidence from the reverse-engineering memo:
- Gmail manually enumerates operations in connectors/gmail/manifest.toml.
- Gmail manually builds introspection in connectors/gmail/src/connector.rs.
- Gmail has service-specific auth/config parsing and refresh-token exchange logic.
- Calendar similarly has manual introspection/catalog shaping and a separate auth parser.
- The destination representation we need already exists in fcp-core/fcp-cli, so the missing piece is the shared Google substrate rather than the consumer side.

The output should be a convergence map with three columns:
1. Must become shared Google substrate.
2. Must remain service-owned handwritten logic.
3. Needs deliberate case-by-case review.

Without this baseline, migration work risks either over-generalizing service-specific semantics or leaving duplicated Google plumbing in place.","acceptance_criteria":"- Exact local duplication points for Gmail and Calendar are recorded in the bead.
- The bead states what the destination types/interfaces already provide.
- A convergence map exists that can block or guide Gmail/Calendar migration work.
- Future implementers do not need to rediscover the same local file-level evidence from scratch.","notes":"This bead is intentionally self-documenting and source-backed. It exists because the migration plan should retain the local evidence that justified it, not just the abstract architectural conclusion.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PearlRidge","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:40:14.669813Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-06T15:18:48.157869Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T15:18:48.157849Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","calendar","fcp2","gmail","google","migration"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.2.7","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:40:14.669813Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":895,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.2.7","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Local repo evidence from the memo that this audit bead must preserve in-line:

- Gmail currently manually enumerates operations in connectors/gmail/manifest.toml.
- Gmail manually builds introspection in connectors/gmail/src/connector.rs.
- Gmail also contains service-specific auth/config logic and refresh-token exchange logic in the connector implementation.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.3","title":"[GOOGLE] Expansion: first wave of missing Google Workspace connectors","description":"After the shared substrate is validated through migrations, use it to ship a focused first wave of high-value Google Workspace connectors with thin service-specific layers and minimal duplicated infrastructure.","design":"The selection for the first expansion wave is driven by strategic value and architectural leverage. Drive, Sheets, Docs, Chat, Workspace Events, People, and Admin Reports all map well to the upstream discovery-driven pattern and together cover a large percentage of practical Google Workspace automation use cases.

The point of this epic is not to create a mega-connector. The point is to use a shared Google platform to make thin, auditable, least-privileged connectors cheap to produce.","acceptance_criteria":"- Each target connector has a self-contained feature issue with service-specific goals, caveats, and acceptance criteria.
- The expansion wave is blocked on migration validation so we do not fork the architecture before it is proven.

Additional Google services can follow once the foundation and first wave prove the pattern.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:29:27.643519Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-06T07:31:19.344878Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","expansion","fcp2","google","workspace"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:29:27.643519Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:31:19.344849Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":888,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk.45.3","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Rationale for target service set:
- Drive, Sheets, Docs, and Chat cover common end-user productivity automations.
- Workspace Events is the key event-driven substrate for Google Workspace workflows.

- Together they create strong combinatorial value when paired with FCP's zone, policy, and introspection model.","created_at":"2026-03-06T07:29:28Z"}]}
